title: Zehneria pallidinervia
taxonomy: Zehneria pallidinervia is classified within the family Cucurbitaceae and the order Cucurbitales. It is a species of flowering plant, which is commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. This plant is known to be closely related to other species within the Cucurbitaceae family, which includes melons, cucumbers, and squash. The classification of Zehneria pallidinervia also places it within the kingdom Plantae, reflecting its status as a plant species. The classification of Zehneria pallidinervia is also subject to change as new research and findings emerge.

intro: Zehneria pallidinervia, also known as the pale-veined cordia, is a type of flowering plant in the family Cucurbitaceae. Traditionally, Zehneria pallidinervia has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and antiviral activities. In herbal medicine, Zehneria pallidinervia is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the production of herbal teas and capsules, as well as being used in traditional remedies for fever and rheumatism.
